claim.content: Truncating the full Bistritzer–MacDonald continuum model for twisted bilayer graphene to the tripod (eight-state) Hamiltonian that retains one low-energy Dirac cone and its three nearest high-energy cones, and then diagonalizing the high-energy block and perturbatively projecting onto the low-energy doublet, yields an effective $2\times2$ Hamiltonian whose renormalized Dirac velocity $v^\ast$ depends on the combination $(A+v_F\Delta K)$. This truncation and perturbative projection (neglecting more distant shells and higher-order couplings) captures the dominant relaxation-induced renormalization of the low-energy Fermi velocity relevant for estimating the shift of the magic condition in the studied twist-angle window.
